all x (european(x) -> exists y (person(y) & travel_free(y, Europe) & right_to_live(y, Europe, x)))  // All Europeans can travel freely within Europe and have the right to live in Europe.
all x (european(x) -> person(x))                                                      // Every European is a person.
all x (person(x) & exists e (right_to_live(x, e) & travel_free(x, e, Europe))) -> 
    (exists y (person(y) & travel_free(y, Europe) & right_to_live(y, Europe, x))))  // Corrected: Every person who has the right to live in Europe *for Europe* can travel freely within Europe.

Hypothesis:
all x (european(x) -> right_to_live(x, Europe))                                          // All Europeans have the right to live in Europe (with 'Europe' explicitly stated, consistent with the premise structure).

Note: The correction in the second premise ensures that the universal quantification over 'e' (for some region e) is properly linked to the existential claim within the same scope, thus fixing the term construction error. The hypothesis remains consistent with the corrected premises.
premise_nl: All Europeans can travel freely within Europe. Every European is a person. Every person who has the right to live in Europe can travel freely within Europe.
hypothesis_nl: All Europeans have the right to live in Europe.
